definition: Enterprise content management system for websites, assets, forms, and headless content. Core product in the Adobe Experience Cloud for digital experience engineering.
Integrated suite of products (CMS, DAM, analytics, personalization) used to design and deliver customer experiences across channels.
Cloud-hosted AEM with continuous delivery, auto-scaling, and Adobe-managed infrastructure. Replaces self-hosted 6.5 for new programs.
Adobe pipeline tool for building, deploying, and monitoring AEM Cloud Service environments (dev, stage, prod).
RESTful OSGi framework AEM uses to map URLs to resources and scripts (JCR nodes).
Java module system for AEM bundles: services, components, and configuration with hot deploy semantics.
Hierarchical content store (Apache Jackrabbit Oak in AEM) holding pages, assets, and configurations.
AEM templating language (formerly Sightly) that separates presentation from logic with automatic XSS context.
Adobe-supported AEM component library with versioning, accessibility, and Cloud Service compatibility.
Caching reverse proxy and security filter in front of AEM publish; invalidation and filter rules are critical ops skills.
Content delivery network caching static and dynamic edge responses; pairs with Dispatcher and EDS.
Adobe edge architecture for ultra-fast sites using document-based authoring and automated performance optimization.
Cross-surface visual editor connecting AEM content to any front-end experience.
Content authored in AEM and delivered via APIs (GraphQL, Content Fragments) to SPAs and native apps.
Structured, channel-neutral content unit with defined models and variations for headless use.
Reusable experience block combining layout and content for consistent omnichannel delivery.
Persisted GraphQL API layer over Content Fragment models for efficient headless queries.
Real-time customer data platform unifying profiles, segments, and activation across Adobe solutions.
System of record for first-party customer data used in personalization and analytics.
A/B testing and personalization engine integrated with AEM and Analytics.
Digital analytics product measuring content and conversion performance on AEM sites.
AEM feature to roll out blueprint sites to regional/live copies with inheritance controls.
Site hierarchy synchronized from a blueprint with configurable inheritance of content and components.
Traditional page-centric authoring in AEM Sites as opposed to headless fragment delivery.
AEM Assets capability for storing, transforming, and governing rich media.
Adobe DAM product within AEM for images, video, metadata, and brand portals.
Enterprise captive center (often in India) building digital platforms for the parent company.
Java annotation-driven API adapting request resources for HTL and JSON exporters.
Dedicated JCR system user for OSGi services performing repository operations with least privilege.
Lucene or property index definition in Oak/JCR affecting query performance in AEM.
